programme.title: PL MOMENTS EP08: ANDY COLE
programme.sub-title: Andy Cole joins Will Perry on the sofa to count down his all-time top ten Premier League Moments.
programme.desc: Andy Cole joins Will Perry on the sofa to count down his all-time top ten Premier League Moments.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP35: ROBERT PIRES
programme.sub-title: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. Having joined the club during Arsene Wenger’s reign, Pires played in one of the greatest sides in the clubs’ history - as well as helping them achieve invincible status in 2003/04. The French winger recalls almost quitting football at a young age and how his mother persuaded him to change his mind, winning the World Cup with France, the difficulties he faced when he first joined the Gunners and what this current Arsenal side need to do to win the Premier League title once again.
programme.desc: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. Having joined the club during Arsene Wenger’s reign, Pires played in one of the greatest sides in the clubs’ history - as well as helping them achieve invincible status in 2003/04. The French winger recalls almost quitting football at a young age and how his mother persuaded him to change his mind, winning the World Cup with France, the difficulties he faced when he first joined the Gunners and what this current Arsenal side need to do to win the Premier League title once again.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP45: TITLE RUN IN: TRUE TURNING POINTS
programme.sub-title: PL Stories takes a look at the forgotten moments that truly defined a Premier League season, offering new perspectives on the games that shaped title races. As the season nears its conclusion, two former Premier League players alongside two football experts revisit iconic yet often overlooked moments, such as Steven Gerrard’s infamous slip in 2014, which many believe ended Liverpool’s title charge. But could it have actually been the following match, when Liverpool let a three-goal lead slip against Crystal Palace, that sealed their fate? Our experts debate these key moments and challenge the common narratives that have shaped Premier League history, sparking fresh conversation on what really determined the outcome of those unforgettable seasons.
programme.desc: PL Stories takes a look at the forgotten moments that truly defined a Premier League season, offering new perspectives on the games that shaped title races. But could it have actually been the following match, when Liverpool let a three-goal lead slip against Crystal Palace, that sealed their fate? Our experts debate these key moments and challenge the common narratives that have shaped Premier League history, sparking fresh conversation on what really determined the outcome of those unforgettable seasons.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP15: SHAY GIVEN: MY NEWCASTLE YEARS
programme.sub-title: Renowned for his lightning-fast reflexes and unwavering courage, Shay Given is one of the Premier League’s all-time greatest goalkeepers. The Irishman reflects on his most memorable moments between the posts as Newcastle’s number one.
programme.desc: Renowned for his lightning-fast reflexes and unwavering courage, Shay Given is one of the Premier League’s all-time greatest goalkeepers. The Irishman reflects on his most memorable moments between the posts as Newcastle’s number one.

programme.title: THE ART OF... EP04: CENTRAL DEFENSIVE MIDFIELDER
programme.sub-title: Owen Hargreaves unpacks The Art of.. The Central Defensive Midfielder, drawing on his time with England and Manchester United. Featuring contributions from Rodri and Claude Makelele as they discuss the position’s dynamics and tactical demands. Plus, a deep dive into Premier League icons like Kante and Fernandinho, players who defined the modern CDM.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP06: THE ENTERTAINERS
programme.sub-title: Nicknamed ‘The Entertainers’, Kevin Keegan’s Newcastle team of the 1995-96 season set the Premier League alight with their confidence and flair that took them agonisingly close to winning their first Premier League title. In this show, we speak to those that were part of that incredible journey and reflect on some of those unforgettable moments that captured hearts and forever changing the club's legacy.

But could it have actually been the following match, when Liverpool let a three-goal lead slip against Crystal Palace, that sealed their fate? Our experts debate these key moments and challenge the common narratives that have shaped Premier League history, sparking fresh conversation on what really determined the outcome of those unforgettable seasons.

programme.title: WELCOME TO THE WEEKEND FRI 11 APR
programme.sub-title: We're on the road exploring the city of Newcastle as Manchester United head to the North East, Forest centre-back Nikola Milenkovic spends three minutes with us, plus find out which English player is a master at speaking Spanish.
programme.desc: We're on the road exploring the city of Newcastle as Manchester United head to the North East, Forest centre-back Nikola Milenkovic spends three minutes with us, plus find out which English player is a master at speaking Spanish.

programme.title: FANZONE FRI 11 APR
programme.sub-title: This is the show that puts international fans at the heart of the Premier League conversation. programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP35: ROBERT PIRES
programme.sub-title: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. Having joined the club during Arsene Wenger’s reign, Pires played in one of the greatest sides in the clubs’ history - as well as helping them achieve invincible status in 2003/04. The French winger recalls almost quitting football at a young age and how his mother persuaded him to change his mind, winning the World Cup with France, the difficulties he faced when he first joined the Gunners and what this current Arsenal side need to do to win the Premier League title once again.
programme.desc: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. Having joined the club during Arsene Wenger’s reign, Pires played in one of the greatest sides in the clubs’ history - as well as helping them achieve invincible status in 2003/04. The French winger recalls almost quitting football at a young age and how his mother persuaded him to change his mind, winning the World Cup with France, the difficulties he faced when he first joined the Gunners and what this current Arsenal side need to do to win the Premier League title once again.

programme.title: FANTASY SHOW FRI 11 APR
programme.sub-title: Saka or Salah? Foden or Fernandes? Wilson or Watkins? Picking your Fantasy team is never easy but this season the Fantasy Show is on hand to help you with those big decisions. programme.title: PL STORIES EP06: THE ENTERTAINERS
programme.sub-title: Nicknamed ‘The Entertainers’, Kevin Keegan’s Newcastle team of the 1995-96 season set the Premier League alight with their confidence and flair that took them agonisingly close to winning their first Premier League title. In this show, we speak to those that were part of that incredible journey and reflect on some of those unforgettable moments that captured hearts and forever changing the club's legacy.

As the season nears its conclusion, two former Premier League players alongside two football experts revisit iconic yet often overlooked moments, such as Steven Gerrard’s infamous slip in 2014, which many believe ended Liverpool’s title charge. But could it have actually been the following match, when Liverpool let a three-goal lead slip against Crystal Palace, that sealed their fate? Our experts debate these key moments and challenge the common narratives that have shaped Premier League history, sparking fresh conversation on what really determined the outcome of those unforgettable seasons.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP35: ROBERT PIRES
programme.sub-title: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. Having joined the club during Arsene Wenger’s reign, Pires played in one of the greatest sides in the clubs’ history - as well as helping them achieve invincible status in 2003/04. The French winger recalls almost quitting football at a young age and how his mother persuaded him to change his mind, winning the World Cup with France, the difficulties he faced when he first joined the Gunners and what this current Arsenal side need to do to win the Premier League title once again.
programme.desc: This week on The Big Interview, two-time Premier League champion, Robert Pires sits down to talk through his career and all things Arsenal. programme.title: PL STORIES EP42: (Subbed) NEURODIVERSITY CELEBRATION
programme.sub-title: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP46: (Subbed) PHILIPPE COUTINHO
programme.sub-title: We take a look at the career of Philippe Coutinho, tracing his journey from a promising young talent to one of the Premier League’s most skilful and dynamic players. We join Coutinho at his current club Vasco da Gama and reflect on his time in Europe, from his rise at Liverpool to his time at Barcelona, Bayern Munich and Aston Villa. Delving into his passion, creativity, and legacy, we celebrate how his flair and vision have left a lasting mark on the Premier League.

Delving into his passion, creativity, and legacy, we celebrate how his flair and vision have left a lasting mark on the Premier League.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP41: (Subbed) ENZO MARESCA – THE ITALIAN JOB
programme.sub-title: A relationship steeped in glory, Chelsea Football Club is the Italian Job. Last summer, Enzo Maresca became the seventh manager from that heritage to take charge at Stamford Bridge. PL Stories looks back at the success shared between the club and managers from Italy, while also delving into Maresca’s own unique coaching journey as he strives to emulate the achievements of those who came before him.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP06: THE ENTERTAINERS
programme.sub-title: Nicknamed ‘The Entertainers’, Kevin Keegan’s Newcastle team of the 1995-96 season set the Premier League alight with their confidence and flair that took them agonisingly close to winning their first Premier League title. In this show, we speak to those that were part of that incredible journey and reflect on some of those unforgettable moments that captured hearts and forever changing the club's legacy.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP45: TITLE RUN IN: TRUE TURNING POINTS
programme.sub-title: PL Stories takes a look at the forgotten moments that truly defined a Premier League season, offering new perspectives on the games that shaped title races. As the season nears its conclusion, two former Premier League players alongside two football experts revisit iconic yet often overlooked moments, such as Steven Gerrard’s infamous slip in 2014, which many believe ended Liverpool’s title charge. But could it have actually been the following match, when Liverpool let a three-goal lead slip against Crystal Palace, that sealed their fate? Our experts debate these key moments and challenge the common narratives that have shaped Premier League history, sparking fresh conversation on what really determined the outcome of those unforgettable seasons.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP42: (Subbed) NEURODIVERSITY CELEBRATION
programme.sub-title: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.
